data_description.pv_actual_sensor: Best practice: a cumulative energy counter (Wh/kWh, total_increasing). If you only have a power reading, use a rolling mean_linear statistics helper (kW) — not a raw instantaneous sensor.
data_description.pv_export_sensor: Cumulative export energy counter (Wh/kWh) preferred; otherwise a rolling mean_linear helper (kW).
data_description.pv_actual_input_mode: Auto-detect picks energy counter vs averaged power from the unit (Wh/kWh vs W/kW).
data_description.pv_export_input_mode: Auto-detect by unit. Energy counter recommended.

database.title: Storage
database.description: History is stored in a built-in file (config/solcast_solar_enhanced.db) — no setup required. Leave enabled for tuning and dampening to work.

data_description.db_retention_days: 0 keeps all history (default). A value bounds the database on low-power devices by deleting older rows daily — but seasonal dampening uses a cross-year window, so keep at least ~400 days (≈13 months) if you rely on it.

data.dampening_gate: Gate dampening until tuning agrees with Solcast orientation

sites.title: Per-site sensor mapping
sites.description: {count} Solcast sites detected. For each array, choose the sensor that measures its generation. If several arrays share one inverter AC output, give each its DC/MPPT sensor too so output can be apportioned. Leave a site blank to skip it. 'Sensor type' is usually Auto-detect.

data_description.pv_actual_sensor: Best practice: a cumulative energy counter (Wh/kWh, total_increasing). If you only have a power reading, use a rolling mean_linear statistics helper (kW) — not a raw instantaneous sensor.
data_description.pv_export_sensor: Cumulative export energy counter (Wh/kWh) preferred; otherwise a rolling mean_linear helper (kW).
data_description.pv_actual_input_mode: Auto-detect picks energy counter vs averaged power from the unit (Wh/kWh vs W/kW).
data_description.pv_export_input_mode: Auto-detect by unit. Energy counter recommended.

database.title: Storage
database.description: History is stored in a built-in file (config/solcast_solar_enhanced.db) — no setup required. Leave enabled for tuning and dampening to work.

data_description.db_retention_days: 0 keeps all history (default). A value bounds the database on low-power devices by deleting older rows daily — but seasonal dampening uses a cross-year window, so keep at least ~400 days (≈13 months) if you rely on it.

data.dampening_gate: Gate dampening until tuning agrees with Solcast orientation

sites.title: Per-site sensor mapping
sites.description: {count} Solcast sites detected. For each array, choose the sensor that measures its generation. If several arrays share one inverter AC output, give each its DC/MPPT sensor too so output can be apportioned. Leave a site blank to skip it. 'Sensor type' is usually Auto-detect.

owm_required.title: OpenWeatherMap needed for PV tuning & dampening
owm_required.description: PV tuning and adaptive dampening use OpenWeatherMap cloud cover to identify clear-sky periods — the only data where measured vs forecast reflects panel geometry and shading rather than passing clouds. OpenWeatherMap is not configured, so every record's cloud cover is unknown and excluded, leaving these features inactive (dampening stays neutral and is not pushed to Solcast). Add a free OpenWeatherMap API key in this integration's options to enable them, or ignore this if you only want history logging.

dampening_gated.title: Dampening paused — tuned orientation differs from Solcast
dampening_gated.description: PV tuning has confidently converged on a panel tilt/azimuth that differs materially from the orientation your Solcast site is configured with. Until they agree, the Solcast forecast is based on the wrong geometry, so adaptive dampening would bake that orientation error into its correction instead of capturing genuine shading. Dampening is therefore held neutral (1.0) for the affected array(s) and nothing is pushed to Solcast. Read the current values from the Tuned Panel Tilt and Tuned Panel Azimuth sensors, update your Solcast site configuration to match, and dampening will resume automatically. To disable this safeguard, turn off ‘Gate dampening…’ in the integration options.
